meta.name: 100-Mile Trail Ultra
meta.subtitle: The ultimate — 100 miles across 36 weeks
meta.description: A 36-week plan for the full 100 miles. The most demanding training plan in the NextSplit library. Built around progressive back-to-back long runs, night running practice, crew coordination, and full-race nutrition rehearsal. Assumes completion of at least a 50-mile ultra first.
meta.coach_notes: One hundred miles will take most runners 24–30 hours. You will run through the night. You will reach moments when stopping seems entirely rational. The training can prepare your legs — it cannot fully prepare your mind. Train with your crew. Practise your drop bags. Know your aid station strategy better than your 5k race pace.

weeks.title: Week 1
weeks.note: Opening block. Build time on feet — pace is irrelevant.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Hill repeats · 6x3min uphill (jog down) · Total 10km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Threshold 20min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Goblet squat 2x12 · Romanian deadlift 2x12 · Reverse lunge 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 2x30s — Your first lower body strength session. Keep loads light — this week is about learning the movements. Lower body strength is the foundation of running economy.

20km trail · Easy · Practice nutrition — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: 14km trail · Back-to-back · Time on tired legs · Key ultra training signal — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Week 2
weeks.note: Second week. Back-to-back fatigue is the training signal.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Hill repeats · 6x3min uphill (jog down) · Total 10km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Threshold 20min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 3x12 · Romanian deadlift 3x12 · Bulgarian split squat 3x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 3x12s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

22km trail · Easy · Practice nutrition — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Hill repeats · 6x3min uphill (jog down) · Total 10km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Threshold 20min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Pull-ups 3x12 · DB row 3x12 · Overhead press 3x12 · Face pulls 3x12 · Core circuit 3x12s — Strong upper body and core keeps your posture tall when you tire. These sessions directly improve arm drive efficiency and reduce the slumping that costs you energy late in a race.

sessions.det: 6km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

Hill repeats · 6x3min uphill (jog down) · Total 10km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Threshold 20min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
16km trail · Easy · Practice nutrition — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Week 18
weeks.note: 50km back-to-back. You are running ultra distances in training.

sessions.det: 8km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.

Hill repeats · 10x3min uphill (jog down) · Total 10km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Threshold 30min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Back squat 4x8 · Romanian deadlift 4x8 · Bulgarian split squat 4x8 · Calf raise 3x15 · Core circuit 4x8s — Lower body strength is the engine of your running economy. These compound lifts build the hip and quad strength that keeps your form together in the final miles.

50km trail · Easy pace · Full kit and nutrition · Night hours if race needs it — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: 30km trail · Back-to-back · Time on tired legs · Key ultra training signal — The long run is the cornerstone of endurance training. Run it easy enough to hold a conversation and you'll finish feeling like you could go further — that's the goal.

nut.d: Porridge with banana. 600ml water. 90–120min before. Gel every 40–45min on runs over 90 minutes. Practise your race nutrition strategy.
Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

weeks.title: Week 19
weeks.note: 52km Saturday — equivalent to a 50-mile race effort.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Keep this genuinely easy. The bulk of your aerobic development happens at low intensity and these runs build the engine that powers your quality sessions.
VO2max · 5x1.5km (3min rec) · Total 11km — Peak-phase intervals — this is where your fitness is tested. These are hard but controlled. If the pace slips, the session is done.
Threshold 35min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold work at peak phase. Your lactate threshold determines the pace you can sustain for an hour. These sessions push that ceiling up.

Pull-ups 4x5 · DB row 4x5 · Overhead press 4x5 · Face pulls 4x5 · Core circuit 4x5s — Upper body work while your legs taper — good timing. Pull movements build the postural strength that holds your form together at race pace.

Full recovery meal within 60 minutes. 30–40g protein, high carbohydrates. Electrolytes if you sweated heavily.

sessions.det: 9km easy · 5:50–6:20/km — Easy effort means conversational pace. This is recovery — your body adapts to training during rest, not during hard sessions.

VO2max · 5x1.5km (3min rec) · Total 11km — Intervals build your VO2max ceiling and teach your body to run efficiently at race pace. Recovery between reps should be easy enough that you can complete every interval at the target pace.

Threshold 35min · Steady sustained effort — Threshold pace is 'comfortably hard' — you could speak in short phrases but not hold a conversation. It builds your ability to sustain faster paces for longer.

Hip thrust 2x12 · Nordic curl 2x12 · Lateral band walk 2x12 · Calf raise 3x15 · Stretching circuit 10min — A gentle posterior chain session and mobility work. During taper this is about keeping the body feeling loose and activated, not adding load.
24km trail · Easy · Practice nutrition — A shorter long run this week — still builds time on feet and aerobic base without adding to accumulated fatigue.

Walk every uphill · Eat at every aid station · Move through the night · Keep forward motion at all costs · The finish is the only goal — Race day. Trust your training. Start conservatively — most races are won or lost in the second half.

nut.d: Large meal 3 hours before. Porridge, banana, nut butter, toast. 800ml water. This is just the beginning of 24+ hours of fuelling.
60–80g carbohydrates per hour. Mix gels and real food from the start. Your ability to absorb nutrition will degrade after 12–15 hours — front-load your fuelling.
At crew points: change socks if wet, assess blisters, eat real food (broth, soup, potatoes, pizza), take 5–10 minutes to reset mentally. Do not sit too long.
Warm food is psychologically vital at night. Broth, ramen, soup at every night station. Caffeine gel or tablet every 3–4 hours through the night.
If solid food is impossible, switch to cola, gels, and broth. Continue electrolytes. The goal is calories in any form.
Eat slowly and consistently for 48 hours. 40g protein at first opportunity. Sleep as soon as medically safe. Continue drinking electrolytes for 24 hours. You have completed something extraordinary.
